name: rancher
description: The Rancher CLI (Command Line Interface) is a unified tool that you can use to interact with Rancher

subcommands.name: apps
subcommands.description: Operations with apps. Uses helm. Flags are prepended with 'helm' can also be accurately described by helm documentation

subcommands.name: ls
subcommands.description: List all apps in the current Rancher server

args.name: APP_NAME/APP_ID
args.description: The name of the app to delete

options.description: Path to an answers file, the format of the file is a map with key:value. This supports JSON and YAML
Path to a helm values file
Namespace to install the app into
Set answers for the template, can be used multiple times. Example: --set foo=bar
Set string answers for the template (Skips Helm's type conversion), can be used multiple times. Example: --set-string foo=bar
Version of the template to use
Suppress asking questions and use the default values when required answers are not provided
Amount of time for helm to wait for k8s commands (default is 300 secs). Example: --helm-timeout 600 (default: 300)
Helm will wait for as long as timeout value, for installed resources to be ready (pods, PVCs, deployments, etc.). Example: --helm-wait

args.name: TEMPLATE_NAME/TEMPLATE_PATH
args.description: Install an app template in the current Rancher server

args.name: APP_NAME
args.description: The name of the app to install

subcommands.name: rollback
subcommands.description: Rollback an app to a previous version

args.name: APP_NAME/APP_ID
args.description: The name/id of the app to rollback

args.name: REVISION_ID/REVISION_NAME
args.description: The name/id of the revision to rollback

subcommands.name: upgrade
subcommands.description: Upgrade an existing app to a newer version

subcommands.name: show-tempate
subcommands.description: Show versions available to install for an app template

subcommands.name: show-app
subcommands.description: Show an app's available versions and revisions

options.description: Output format: 'json', 'yaml' or custom format
Only display IDs or suppress help text
Show help

subcommands.name: ls
subcommands.description: List all catalogs in the current Rancher server

options.description: Output format: 'json', 'yaml' or custom format
Only display IDs or suppress help text
Show help
Branch from the url to use (default: 'master')
Version of helm the app(s) in your catalog will use for deployment. Use 'v2' for helm 2 or 'v3' for helm 3 (default: 'v2')

args.name: CATALOG_NAME
args.description: The name of the catalog to add

args.name: CATALOG_URL
args.description: The URL of the catalog to add

subcommands.name: globaldns
subcommands.description: Operations on global DNS providers and entries

options.description: API Bearer Token to authenticate Rancher CLI to your Rancher server

args.name: SERVER_URL
args.description: The server url you want to login to

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: ⚠️ Delete a server from the local config

args.name: SERVER_NAME
args.description: The name of server you want to delete

args.name: SERVER_NAME
args.description: The name of server you want to switch to

subcommands.name: settings
subcommands.description: Show settings for the current server

args.name: SETTING_NAME
args.description: The name of setting you want to get

subcommands.name: set
subcommands.description: Set the value for a setting

options.description: Output format: 'json', 'yaml' or custom format
Only display IDs or suppress help text
Show help
Reset the setting back to it's default value. If the default value is (blank) it will be set to that

args.name: SETTINGNAME
args.description: The name of setting you want to set

args.name: SETTING_VALUE
args.description: The value of setting you want to set

subcommands.name: wait
subcommands.description: Wait for resources cluster, app, project, multiClusterApp

subcommands.name: token
subcommands.description: Authenticate and generate new kubeconfig token

subcommands.name: help
subcommands.description: Shows a list of commands or help for one command

options.description: Debug logging
Path to rancher config (default: `$home/.rancher`) [$RANCHER_CONFIG_DIR]
Show help for rancher cli
Print the version
